Typeguard: [Remove any types in MobilePreview] (#973)

* Typeguard: [Remove any types in MobilePreview]

What: Replaced the `config: any` type in `MobilePreview` with `config: unknown` and removed the `(block: any)` typing from the `content.map` iteration.
Risk: Using `any` bypassed TypeScript's checking, which could lead to missed regressions if the underlying Zod schema (which acts as a runtime type guard) changes its structure.
Fix: Typed the incoming dynamic data prop as `unknown`. Since it is immediately validated by `PageConfigSchema.safeParse`, the `content.map` callback now natively infers `block` directly from the inferred Zod type. Also removed unnecessary `// biome-ignore` directives.
Scope: `apps/web/src/components/dashboard/mobile-preview.tsx`

Co-authored-by: ogabasseyy <196925138+ogabasseyy@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(web): replace z.any() with z.unknown() in PageConfigSchema theme

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix issue label deletion with Actions tokens (#37013)

Use shared repo permission resolution for Actions task users in issue
label remove and clear paths, and add a regression test for deleting
issue labels with a Gitea Actions token.

This fixes issue label deletion when the request is authenticated with a
Gitea Actions token.
Fixes #37011 

The bug was that the delete path re-resolved repository permissions
using the normal user permission helper, which does not handle Actions
task users. As a result, `DELETE
/api/v1/repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/{index}/labels/{id}` could return
`500` for Actions tokens even though label listing and label addition
worked.

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Fix cursor overshoot by serializing SSH input writes

Replace frontend ACK-based input queue with backend serialization.
Each SSH session now has a dedicated write task that processes input
sequentially, eliminating concurrent channel writes that scrambled
input order. Frontend sends fire-and-forget, backend guarantees ordering.

Fixes intermittent cursor position errors during rapid arrow key edits.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

COE-307: Normalize LiteLLM request data into canonical request tables (#20)

* feat(normalization): implement request normalizer job with reconciliation reporting

Summary:
- Create normalize_requests.py with RequestNormalizer and RequestNormalizerJob classes
- Implement ReconciliationReport for tracking unmapped rows with actionable diagnostics
- Add UnmappedRowDiagnostics for detailed per-row failure analysis
- Wire normalization job into CLI with `benchmark normalize run` and `benchmark normalize report` commands
- Export new classes from collectors package
- Add comprehensive unit tests (30 tests covering normalization, reporting, and job execution)

Rationale:
- LiteLLM request data needs normalization into canonical Request ORM entities
- Session correlation keys (experiment_id, variant_id, etc.) must be preserved in metadata
- Idempotent bulk insert via SQLRequestRepository prevents duplicates
- Reconciliation report surfaces unmapped rows with missing field counts and error categories
- CLI integration enables operator-driven normalization workflows

Implementation Details:
- RequestNormalizer handles timestamp parsing (ISO format, Unix epoch, multiple field names)
- Supports latency extraction (seconds->ms conversion, multiple field variants)
- Token counts extracted from nested usage object or top-level fields
- Error detection from error field or response.error
- Cache hit detection from cache_hit or cached fields
- Correlation keys searched in both top-level and nested metadata

Tests:
- All 30 new normalization tests pass
- All 210 existing tests pass
- Lint clean on all new files

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(demo): Address PR #10 feedback - fix demo_session_service.py runtime errors (#21)

* COE-305: Implement repository layer and benchmark metadata services

## Repository Layer (src/benchmark_core/repositories/)

New SQLAlchemy-based repositories for all canonical entities:
- base.py: AbstractRepository, SQLAlchemyRepository, RepositoryError hierarchy
- provider_repository.py: SQLProviderRepository with model management
- variant_repository.py: SQLVariantRepository with filtering by provider/harness
- experiment_repository.py: SQLExperimentRepository with variant associations
- task_card_repository.py: SQLTaskCardRepository with search capabilities
- harness_profile_repository.py: SQLHarnessProfileRepository
- session_repository.py: SQLSessionRepository with safe session creation/finalization
- request_repository.py: SQLRequestRepository with idempotent batch operations

## Service Layer (src/benchmark_core/services/)

New services for benchmark metadata management:
- session_service.py: Safe session lifecycle (create/finalize) with validation
- provider_service.py: Provider configuration management with model aliases
- variant_service.py: Variant definition and benchmark tags
- experiment_service.py: Experiment grouping and variant associations
- task_card_service.py: Task definition management
- harness_profile_service.py: Harness configuration with env rendering
- credential_service.py: Session-scoped proxy credential issuance
- benchmark_metadata_service.py: Unified service for cross-entity operations

## Key Features

- Referential integrity preserved via explicit FK checks and error handling
- Duplicate identifier rejection with DuplicateIdentifierError
- Safe session lifecycle: create with validation, finalize atomically
- Idempotent batch request ingestion for collectors
- Full test coverage: 52 new tests, all passing (129 total)

Renamed existing files for backward compatibility:
- repositories.py -> repositories_abc.py
- services.py -> services_abc.py

Co-authored-by: openhands <openhands@all-hands.dev>

Co-authored-by: openhands <openhands@all-hands.dev>

* COE-305: Add runtime evidence demo script and evidence output

- Add demo_session_service.py to demonstrate acceptance criteria
- Add EVIDENCE_COE305.md with runtime verification output
- All 3 acceptance criteria verified via live demonstration

* COE-305: Fix mypy type errors in repository layer - add CursorResult casting

* COE-305: Fix merge conflicts and repository compatibility issues

- Merge origin/main into COE-305 branch
- Fix SQLSessionRepository.finalize_session() to accept ended_at parameter
- Fix UUID handling in create_session_safe for SQLAlchemy compatibility
- Update CLI to use SQLSessionRepository consistently
- Fix test_services to use SQLSessionRepository in fixture
- Fix session.id references to session.session_id in tests
- All 180 tests now pass

* COE-305: Fix CollectionJobService to use create_many instead of create_batch

* fix(demo): correct demo_session_service.py runtime errors

Summary:
- Wrap SQLAlchemy Session in SQLSessionRepository before passing to SessionService
- Use session.session_id instead of session.id (domain model uses session_id field)
- Add timezone-aware/naive datetime handling for SQLite compatibility
- Import SQLSessionRepository in demo script

Rationale:
The demo script was crashing with AttributeError because SessionService expects
a SQLSessionRepository but received a raw SQLAlchemy Session. Additionally, the
Session domain model uses 'session_id' field (not 'id'), causing AttributeError
when accessing session.id. The datetime comparison also failed due to SQLite
storing naive timestamps while Python datetime.now(UTC) creates aware timestamps.

These fixes address outstanding critical feedback from PR #10 that was merged
before all runtime issues were resolved.

Tests:
- Ran scripts/demo_session_service.py - all 3 demos pass
- Ran pytest tests/unit/test_services.py - 32 tests pass

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
security(proxy): add Origin-based CSRF protection in middleware (#899)

Replace per-route checkCsrfProtection() token pattern with a single
Origin header check in the middleware layer. This is the 2026 best
practice: modern browsers always send Origin on cross-origin mutation
requests, and SameSite=Lax cookies (Supabase default) provide
defense-in-depth.

Benefits:
- Covers ALL API routes automatically (vs ~100 individual calls)
- Zero client-side changes (no buildCsrfHeaders() wiring needed)
- No risk of breaking callers that don't send x-csrf-token
- Existing per-route checks remain as redundant defense-in-depth

Skip list: Bearer auth (mobile), webhooks, auth callbacks, cron jobs,
public analytics. Custom merchant domains are verified via domain cache.

Closes #898 (replaced approach — that PR would have broken logout and
loyalty redemption by requiring CSRF tokens callers don't send).

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

tasks contest the same files:

    "internal/tools/session.go: 2 tasks (T-1, T-2)"

Complements existing ownership_warnings for parallel safety.

Co-authorized-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat: P1/P2 - Dependency awareness, stale detection, build results

Adds ambient intelligence to system context:

**Dependency chain awareness:**
- Warns when In Progress tasks have unmet dependencies
- Shows blocked tasks in system prompt

**Stale task detection:**
- Tasks >7 days old flagged with age
- Appears in system context automatically

**Build/test result awareness:**
- shell.execute.after hook captures test/build output
- Toast on success: "✅ Tests passed"
- Toast on failure: "❌ Failed (exit 1): error details"

feat(predict): add shape parameter for non-square inference (#866)

* feat(predict): add shape parameter for non-square inference (#682)

RFDETR.predict() previously ignored a shape kwarg and always resized
inputs to the square (resolution, resolution) — making it impossible to
run inference matching a non-square ONNX export. Add an explicit
shape: Optional[tuple[int, int]] parameter that, when provided, overrides
the default square resize, consistent with the existing export(shape=…) API.

Also fix the _optimized_resolution mismatch guard to compare both height
and width (previously only height was checked), and validate that any
provided shape has both dimensions divisible by 14 (same constraint as
export()).

- Add Raises: section to predict() docstring covering all four
  ValueError paths introduced by the shape parameter
- Document the divisible-by-14 constraint in the shape param description
- Reject bool values in shape validation (bool is a subclass of int
  but semantically invalid as a dimension)
- Add blank line between shape validation block and the inference
  optimisation warning for readability
- Remove stale # type: ignore[misc] — Optional[tuple[int, int]] is
  sufficiently typed after the `if shape is not None:` guard
- Add 8 parametrized test cases covering float dims, wrong arity
  (1- and 3-element), zero/negative, and bool dimensions
- Add CHANGELOG entry under [Unreleased] ### Added (closes #682)
- Add match="shape" to test_predict_shape_invalid_raises so any
  ValueError unrelated to the shape parameter cannot silently pass
- Add negative_width case (-14 width) to complete the negative-dim
  symmetry with existing negative_height
- Parametrize test_predict_shape_not_divisible_by_14_raises to cover
  height-not-divisible path alongside the existing width path
- Apply ruff type-annotation modernisation (Optional[...] → ... | None,
  bare tuple → tuple[int | float | bool, ...])
- Add support for integer-like types (e.g., numpy, torch) via the `__index__` protocol.
- Improve shape validation error messages and clarify bool rejection.
- Update test coverage to ensure acceptance of integer-like dimensions.

---------

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(storefront): return 404 for unmatched paths and normalize blog redirect (#875)

* fix(storefront): return 404 for unmatched paths and normalize blog redirect

Invalid storefront paths (e.g., /product/some-slug, /product-tag/samsung)
were falling through to the homepage and returning 200 instead of 404.
This caused soft-404 SEO issues. Fix reads x-pathname header to detect
when no child route matched and calls notFound().

Also normalizes malformed blog.ogabassey.com paths that contain an
accidental domain prefix (e.g., /ogabassey.com/blog/...) before
redirecting, preventing double-domain URLs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add missing headers import for storefront 404 check

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: trigger CI and CodeRabbit review

* fix(proxy): handle blog redirect edge cases

- Bare domain path (/ogabassey.com) now redirects to /blog/ instead of
  producing a broken URL
- Paths already containing /blog/ are stripped to prevent double /blog/blog/

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(proxy): use explicit domain allowlist instead of regex

Replace overmatch-prone dotted-segment regex with an explicit Set of
known accidental domain prefixes. Prevents /favicon.ico or /sitemap.xml
from being misidentified as domain prefixes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(proxy): derive root domain dynamically instead of hardcoding

Strip the blog. prefix from the hostname to get the root domain,
so the accidental-prefix detection adapts automatically without
maintaining a hardcoded allowlist.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(storefront): handle path-mode homepage in 404 check

In dev path-mode, x-pathname is /{slug} for the homepage (not /).
Allow both / and /{slug} as valid homepage pathnames to prevent
a false 404 in development.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

🛡️ Sentinel: [MEDIUM] Fix timing attack vulnerability on cron secret endpoints (#877)

* 🛡️ Sentinel: [MEDIUM] Fix timing attack vulnerability in cron APIs

Replaced strict string inequality checks (`!==`) on cron secrets with constant-time `crypto.timingSafeEqual` to prevent timing attacks across multiple `apps/web/src/app/api/cron/*` endpoints. Added preliminary length checks before comparison to avoid unhandled buffer length exceptions in Node's crypto library.

Co-authored-by: ogabasseyy <196925138+ogabasseyy@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(cron): align cleanup-orders auth with x-cron-secret pattern and add missing blank line

  header to match all other cron endpoints
- Add blank line before heading in sentinel.md for proper markdown formatting
- Add cleanup-orders to vercel.json crons array (daily at 2 AM)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* 🛡️ Sentinel: [MEDIUM] Fix timing attack vulnerability in cron APIs

Replaced strict string inequality checks (`!==`) on cron secrets with constant-time `crypto.timingSafeEqual` to prevent timing attacks across multiple `apps/web/src/app/api/cron/*` endpoints. Added preliminary length checks before comparison to avoid unhandled buffer length exceptions in Node's crypto library.

Co-authored-by: ogabasseyy <196925138+ogabasseyy@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(feed): use canonical category-based product URLs in feeds (#876)

Google Merchant and OpenAI feeds were generating incorrect product URLs.
GMC hardcoded /products/{slug} which 301-redirects (penalized by Google).
OpenAI feed incorrectly included merchant slug in URL path and used raw
category text instead of proper slugs.

Both feeds now use buildProductUrl() from seo-utils.ts which generates
the canonical /{category-slug}/{product-slug} pattern. The OpenAI feed
also resolves custom domains via buildMerchantBaseUrl() instead of
deriving URLs from the request host header.

feat(rankclaw): bootstrap repo and delivery workflow

Summary:
- initialize rankclaw from the PI-Starter base and rename workspace
  identities for the product repo
- add Stage 1 artifacts including PRD0, PRD, delivery docs, and
  evaluation templates for the approved SEO plan
- add Stage 2 support files including the Linear story template,
  backlog index, and review-oriented GitHub templates

Rationale:
- establish the canonical repo baseline before Symphony execution so
  future stories have a stable product and process contract
- keep the three-stage approval model explicit in-repo so backlog and
  execution changes remain gated by review
- make the repo ready for the first CLI-focused implementation story
  without starting broader product work prematurely

Tests:
- npm run docs:list
- npm test
- npm run check
- npm run agent:check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(storefront): use headers() routing mode for /products redirect (#868)

* fix(web): eliminate dynamic calls from storefront pages for PPR compatibility

Remove headers(), cookies(), and draftMode() calls from storefront page
components and generateMetadata functions so Next.js 16 can generate static
shells with Dynamic IO (cacheComponents: true). This eliminates ~50+/hr
"Expected the resume data" errors in production.

Key changes:
- New buildStoreUrl() utility replaces all headers()-based URL construction
- Two-tier proxy URL normalization replaces per-page lowercase redirects
- Dead cookie reads removed from 5 pages (wallet, imei-check, repairs, swap, category)
- Theme cookie reads removed from layout + homepage (V2ThemeProvider handles client-side)
- Blog post page wrapped in Suspense; canonical URL fix for doubled-slug bug
- Cart cookies() moved inside Suspense boundary
- Blog listing + pages/blog switched to getPublicSupabaseClient()
- Layout keeps minimal headers() for routing mode (consistently dynamic, no PPR errors)
- .avif added atomically to STATIC_FILES_REGEX, IMAGE_FILES_REGEX, config.matcher
- CodeRabbit review fixes: dead vars, vi globals scoped to test overrides,
  afterEach cleanup, notFound assertion, unused slug prop, full-page fallback

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(storefront): address CodeRabbit review findings on PPR branch

- Pass postUrl to BlogPostBody to prevent doubled-slug URLs on subdomains
- Add error handling for cart VAT settings query
- Fix domain-identifier fallback in buildStoreUrl for custom domain slugs
- Expand layout theme comment explaining PPR trade-off
- Add NEXT_PUBLIC_ROOT_DOMAIN test for buildStoreUrl
- Add BlogPostPageFallback snapshot test
- Delegate pages/blog to getCachedBlogListing instead of raw Supabase query
- Extract duplicated getSeoData helper in category page

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(storefront): address reviewer follow-up findings

- Use toBeInTheDocument() instead of toBeDefined() in BlogPostPageFallback test
- Add inline comment documenting NODE_ENV redirect limitation for Vercel preview
- Add Blog + BreadcrumbList JSON-LD structured data to blog listing page

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(storefront): use headers()-based routing mode for /products redirect

Replace NODE_ENV check with proxy header detection for the /products/[slug]
category redirect. NODE_ENV=development incorrectly assumed path-mode routing,
breaking Vercel preview where NODE_ENV=production but routing is path-based.

The /products route is explicitly force-dynamic since it mostly returns 308
redirects — PPR offers near-zero benefit for redirect-only responses.

For [category]/[productSlug], keep NODE_ENV check with documented limitation
to preserve PPR on the common render path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(storefront): remove force-dynamic incompatible with cacheComponents

Route segment config `dynamic` is not allowed when `nextConfig.cacheComponents`
is enabled. The `headers()` call already opts this route into dynamic rendering
automatically.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(storefront): unify product schema offers and breadcrumb URLs via getProductUrl (#871)

Previously, offers.url used a hardcoded /products/ path while breadcrumbs
used the category-based path from getProductUrl(). This sent conflicting
canonical signals to search engines. Now both use getProductUrl() for
consistent SEO signals.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(aug): correct degenerate `translate_percent` range in AUG_AGGRESSIVE (#863)

(0.1, 0.1) is a degenerate range that forces Albumentations Affine to
always shift right/down by exactly 10%; never left or up. Corrected to
(-0.1, 0.1) for symmetric bidirectional translation, consistent with the
signed companion parameters rotate: (-15, 15) and shear: (-5, 5).

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>
